metadata.note: Resmetirom, VK2809, ALG-055009, and HSK-31679 fully verified.

compounds.company: Shanghai Institute of Materia Medica (SIMM, Chinese Academy of Sciences)

chemical_identifiers.notes: No public data found. Likely early-stage/preclinical THR-β agonist. May be internal designation for compound in discovery/optimization phase. Related to SIMM's 2023 J Med Chem publication (PMID: 36799411) describing 'compound 15' with excellent liver-to-serum ratio (93:1), higher potency/selectivity than resmetirom.

pubmed_papers.note: No direct publications for ZB-129. Related SIMM research: Hu L, et al. 'Discovery of Highly Potent and Selective Thyroid Hormone Receptor β Agonists for the Treatment of Nonalcoholic Steatohepatitis.' J Med Chem. 2023;66(5):3284-3300. PMID: 36799411 - describes 'compound 15' with excellent liver-to-serum ratio (93:1), higher potency/selectivity than MGL-3196 (resmetirom).

compounds.key_findings: No public data found for ZB-129. Comprehensive search of PubChem (no CID), ClinicalTrials.gov (no trials), PubMed (no direct publications), Google Patents (no patents) returned no results. SIMM (Shanghai Institute of Materia Medica, Chinese Academy of Sciences) verified company with active NASH drug discovery program, particularly THR-β agonists. ZB-129 appears to be early-stage/preclinical THR-β agonist, possibly internal designation for compound in discovery/optimization phase. Related to SIMM's 2023 J Med Chem publication (PMID: 36799411) describing 'compound 15' with excellent liver-to-serum ratio (93:1), higher potency/selectivity than resmetirom.
compounds.notes: No public data. Early-stage/preclinical. Related to J Med Chem 2023 (PMID: 36799411).

chemical_identifiers.notes: Selective thyroid hormone receptor beta (THR-β) agonist. Brand name: REZDIFFRA®

clinical_trials.note: Primary efficacy trial for FDA approval

patents.note: THR-β agonist compounds, pharmaceutical compositions, and methods of treating NASH/MASH. Owned by Madrigal Pharmaceuticals. Patent search: https://patents.google.com/?q=Resmetirom+Madrigal

pubmed_papers.title: A Phase 3, Randomized, Controlled Trial of Resmetirom in NASH with Liver Fibrosis (MAESTRO-NASH)
Resmetirom for nonalcoholic fatty liver disease: a randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled phase 3 trial (MAESTRO-NAFLD-1)
Resmetirom (MGL-3196) for the treatment of non-alcoholic steatohepatitis: a multicentre, randomised, double-blind, placebo-controlled, phase 2 trial

compounds.key_findings: Resmetirom (REZDIFFRA®) is the FIRST FDA-approved THR-β agonist for MASH/NASH (Mar 14, 2024). MAESTRO-NASH Phase 3 trial showed 25.9-29.9% NASH resolution vs 9.7% placebo (p<0.001), and 24.2-25.9% fibrosis improvement vs 14.2% placebo (p<0.001). Secondary endpoints: LDL-C reduction -13.6% to -16.3%, hepatic fat reduction -34.9% to -38.6%. FDA designations: Breakthrough Therapy, Fast Track, Priority Review. 373+ PubMed publications. EMA approved August 2025.
compounds.notes: FDA Approved Mar 14, 2024. First treatment for NASH/MASH with liver fibrosis (F2-F3). Brand name: REZDIFFRA®. 80mg and 100mg oral tablets, once daily. Most common AEs: diarrhea (23-33%), nausea (15-18%).

chemical_identifiers.notes: Oral, once-daily, highly selective liver-targeting THR-β full agonist. No PubChem CID yet (typical for early clinical).

clinical_trials.note: Adults with MRI-based or clinically defined MASH
Adjunct to semaglutide in adults with obesity

pubmed_papers.note: No PubMed publications yet. Phase 1 data presented at ADA 2024 (late-breaking poster) and AASLD November 2024 (two late-breaking abstracts). Typical for early clinical development.

compounds.key_findings: ECC-4703 is an oral, once-daily, highly selective liver-targeting THR-β full agonist from Eccogene (Boston/Shanghai, dual headquarters). Phase 2a MOSAIC trial (MASH) FDA cleared IND November 2024. Phase 2a MIST trial (obesity adjunct to semaglutide) first patient enrolled November 2024. Phase 1 data presented at ADA 2024 and AASLD November 2024. Improved PK/PD and safety profiles. Suitable for combination with GLP-1 RAs. Eccogene licensed ECC5004/AZD5004 (oral GLP-1 RA) to AstraZeneca October 2024 ($60M milestone). Other pipeline: ECC0509 (SSAO inhibitor for OA Pain/MASH) Phase 2a.
compounds.notes: Phase 2a. MOSAIC (MASH) & MIST (obesity) trials. ADA 2024, AASLD 2024 presentations.

chemical_identifiers.notes: No public data found. Company not found in public databases.

Company 'Kaisi Kaidi Pharmaceuticals' not found in public databases. Compound may be very early-stage/preclinical or information not yet publicly disclosed.

chemical_identifiers.notes: GalNAc-modified THR-β agonist with hepatocyte-targeted delivery (ASGPR ligands)

patents.note: No specific patent documents identified in public searches. Patent applications may be pending.

pubmed_papers.title: KYLO-0603, a novel liver-targeting, thyroid hormone receptor-β agonist for the inhibition of MASH progression

compounds.key_findings: Kylo-0603 is a liver-targeting THR-β agonist from Kylonova Biopharma (Xiamen, China). Preclinical data (PLoS One 2025, PMID: 40953087) shows: serum cholesterol ↓69.2%, LDL-C ↓88.2%, steatosis -1.3 points (P<0.001), inflammation -1.8 points (P<0.0001), ballooning -0.8 points (P<0.01), NAS score -3.7 points (P<0.0001), fibrosis -0.6 points (P<0.05). GalNAc-modified with ASGPR ligands for hepatocyte-targeted delivery. Not yet in human clinical trials. 1 PubMed publication + 1 bioRxiv preprint.
compounds.notes: Preclinical. PubChem CID 177823044. 1 PubMed paper (PLoS One 2025). Liver-targeting THR-β agonist.

compounds.stage: Phase 1 (FDA IND Feb 2024, NMPA IND June 2024)

chemical_identifiers.notes: THR-β selective agonist. Oral tablet formulation.

clinical_trials.note: FDA IND approved Feb 19, 2024. NMPA (CDE) IND approved June 18, 2024 (CXHL2400331). Phase 1 recruitment ongoing in Beijing.

patents.note: Company reports 33 trademark registrations. Specific CS-060304 patents not publicly disclosed.

compounds.key_findings: CS-060304 (Thybetaxor) is a THR-β selective agonist from Kaisi Kaidi Pharmaceuticals (Shanghai, China) for NASH/MASH and lipid lowering. FDA IND approved Feb 19, 2024. NMPA IND approved June 18, 2024 (CXHL2400331). Phase 1 ongoing. Preclinical data shows excellent lipid-lowering effects and good safety profile. Company completed Series C funding (~500M CNY) in 2026. Pipeline includes 10+ drug candidates including Linafexor (FXR agonist).
compounds.notes: Phase 1. FDA IND Feb 2024, NMPA IND June 2024. Series C 2026.

chemical_identifiers.notes: Kanghong primarily known for ophthalmology drugs (conbercept/KH902 for AMD/DME). No public data for KH-629 as THR-beta agonist.

compounds.key_findings: No public data found for KH-629 as THR-beta agonist. Kanghong Pharmaceutical (Chengdu, China) is primarily known for ophthalmology drugs, specifically conbercept (KH902) - an approved anti-VEGF therapy for AMD, DME, and retinal diseases. 24+ PubMed publications exist for conbercept, but ZERO for KH-629. Compound may be early preclinical, internal code, or confidential THR-beta program not yet disclosed.
compounds.notes: No public data. Kanghong known for ophthalmology (conbercept), not NASH.

compounds.key_findings: No public data found for ASC-43F. Comprehensive search of PubChem (no CID), ClinicalTrials.gov (no trials), EU Clinical Trials Register (no trials), PubMed (0 results), Europe PMC (no results), Google Patents (no patents), PR Newswire, GlobeNewswire, Chemical databases (ChemWhat, LookChem, Chemsrc) returned no results. Ascletis Pharma (1672.HK, Hong Kong) verified company with focus on metabolic diseases. Key technologies: AISBDD, ULAP, POTENT platforms. Publicly disclosed pipeline: ASC30, ASC35, ASC36, ASC37, ASC39 (weight management programs). ASC-43F appears to be either preclinical candidate, internal compound code, or THR-β agonist program in early development. Information may only be available through specialized paid databases (Cortellis, Pharmaprojects) or direct company contact.

compounds.key_findings: KPC000154 is a THR-β selective agonist from KPC Pharmaceuticals (昆药集团, 600422.SH) for NASH/MASH. NMPA IND approved Nov 2024 (2024LP02631, 2024LP02632). Phase I clinical trial service tender launched Nov 2024. Supplemental application accepted Jan 9, 2026 (CXHB2600010). KPC is a top 100 Chinese pharma company (market cap ~9.2B CNY, revenue ~9B CNY). Major shareholder: China Resources Sanjiu (华润三九) since Dec 2022. No efficacy data or publications yet (Phase I stage).
compounds.notes: Phase 1. NMPA IND Nov 2024. KPC Pharma (600422.SH, ~9.2B CNY market cap).

chemical_identifiers.notes: Liver-selective TRβ agonist prodrug (HepDirect technology). Also known as MB07811.

patents.note: HepDirect prodrug technology patents. Key patents: Erion MD et al. PNAS 2007;104(39):15490-15495 (PMID: 17878314). Viking Therapeutics holds patents on TRβ agonists and liver-targeting thyromimetics.

pubmed_papers.title: A Liver-Specific Thyromimetic, VK2809, Decreases Hepatosteatosis in Glycogen Storage Disease Type Ia
Targeting Thyroid Hormone Receptor-Beta Agonists to the Liver Reduces Cholesterol and Triglycerides
Development and validation for bioanalysis of VK2809

compounds.key_findings: VK2809 is a liver-selective TRβ agonist prodrug (HepDirect technology) from Viking Therapeutics. Phase 2b VOYAGE trial showed 38-55% hepatic fat reduction (12 weeks), 63-75% NASH resolution vs 29% placebo (52 weeks), 44-57% fibrosis improvement vs 34% placebo. LDL-C reduction 11-20% placebo-adjusted. 94% of AEs mild/moderate, excellent GI tolerability. Mechanism: liver-targeted prodrug cleaved by CYP450 to active TRβ agonist (MB07344). 11 PubMed publications.
compounds.notes: Phase 2b completed. VOYAGE trial (NCT04173065). Also known as MB07811. HepDirect prodrug technology minimizes cardiac/thyroid effects.

patents.note: Specific ALG-055009 patent numbers not verified in public searches.

pubmed_papers.title: Discovery and Preclinical Profile of ALG-055009, a Potent and Selective Thyroid Hormone Receptor Beta (THR-β) Agonist for the Treatment of MASH

A multipart phase 1 study of the safety, pharmacodynamics and pharmacokinetics of ALG-055009, a novel thyroid hormone receptor beta (THR-β) agonist for metabolic dysfunction-associated steatohepatitis (MASH), in healthy participants

compounds.key_findings: ALG-055009 is a selective THR-β agonist from Aligos Therapeutics. Phase 1 completed (Clin Pharmacol Drug Dev 2026). Dose-dependent decreases in ApoB and LDL-C observed. Increases in SHBG. Half-life ~20 hours, rapid absorption, low variability. Well tolerated with mostly mild/moderate AEs. NO hepatic fat/NASH/fibrosis data yet (Phase 2 not started). 2 PubMed publications (2024, 2026).
compounds.notes: Phase 1 completed. Phase 2 not yet started. Lipid lowering (ApoB, LDL-C) and SHBG increase verified. No histological efficacy data available yet.

compounds.key_findings: ASC-41 is a THR-β agonist from Ascletis Pharma (1672.HK). Reported Phase 2a for NASH/MASH. Specific efficacy data, trial numbers, and publications not accessible due to website restrictions (404 errors, Cloudflare blocks). Company focuses on metabolic diseases with AI-assisted drug discovery platform.
compounds.notes: Phase 2a reported but not verified. Access restrictions on company website and clinical trial databases.

clinical_trials.note: 12-week efficacy trial in Asian patients with MASLD

patents.note: Haisco Pharmaceutical has filed patents for THR-β agonists. Specific HSK-31679 patent numbers not verified in public searches.

pubmed_papers.title: Efficacy and Safety of HSK31679 in Asian Patients With MASLD: A Randomized Controlled Trial

compounds.key_findings: HSK-31679 is a THR-β agonist from Haisco Pharmaceutical. Phase 2a trial (NCT05795517) showed significant hepatic fat reduction: -14.0% (40mg), -22.7% (80mg), -29.2% (160mg) vs -4.1% placebo (12 weeks). 80mg and 160mg doses statistically significant (p=0.002 and p<0.001). Well tolerated with diarrhea as most common TEAE (26-38% vs 4.8% placebo). No drug-related SAEs. 1 PubMed publication (Clin Gastroenterol Hepatol 2026).
compounds.notes: Phase 2a completed. NCT05795517 verified. Hepatic fat reduction -22.7% to -29.2% at 80-160mg doses. Well tolerated.

chemical_identifiers.notes: Highly selective THR-β agonist (23-fold vs THR-α)

clinical_trials.note: 12-week, randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled, multicenter Phase 2a study in MASH

pubmed_papers.title: TERN-501 monotherapy and combination therapy with TERN-101 in metabolic dysfunction-associated steatohepatitis: the randomized phase 2a DUET trial

compounds.key_findings: TERN-501 is a highly selective THR-β agonist (23-fold vs THR-α) from Terns Pharmaceuticals (TERN, NASDAQ) for MASH/NASH. Phase 2a DUET trial completed. Dose-dependent, significant reductions in MRI-PDFF: -15.4% (1mg), -27.5% (3mg, P=0.0036), -44.8% (6mg, P<0.0001) vs placebo -4.0%. Incidence of AEs similar to placebo. PubChem CID 146402726. Molecular formula C17H15Cl2N5O5. Published in Nature Medicine July 2025 (PMID: 40500414).
compounds.notes: Phase 2a completed. PubChem CID 146402726. Nature Medicine 2025.
